# PreScale

> PreScale is an open-source command-line load tester. Point it at any URL and it ramps virtual
> users, finds the route that breaks first, and tells you in plain English why it failed and how
> to fix it. Local-first, deterministic (no LLM in the core path), Apache-2.0.

## What it does
- Ramps closed-loop virtual users over HTTP/2 — no external load tool or server to stand up.
- Finds the first route to break and the concurrency where it starts to give.
- Diagnoses the bottleneck (connection pool, concurrency ceiling, slow query) and names the fix.
- Reports a plain-English verdict, not a dashboard.
- Runs on your machine; your data never leaves it.

## Install
- `pip install prescale` (requires Python 3.10+)

## Commands
- `prescale investigate <url>` — find the first breaking point, then probe it to explain why.
- `prescale run <url>` — load test with a live ramp table (users, throughput, p50/p95/p99, errors).

## How it compares
- Locust and k6 are load engines you script (Python / JavaScript). PreScale is zero-config: point
  it at a URL and read the verdict. Reach for PreScale for a pre-launch readiness check.

## Links
- Site: https://prescale.dev/
- Docs: https://prescale.dev/#/docs
- Source: https://github.com/pyjeebz/prescale
- PyPI: https://pypi.org/project/prescale/
